Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

I need to learn mrdanga

Other · asked by user [] · 2007-05-24 · 9 answers
I need to learn mrdanga, is there any facility online?
user [2]
http://chantandbehappy.com/drum/
http://mrdanga.wordpress.com/
user [255]
Mishra,
Haribol and dundavat pranamas Prabhuji. I am so happy that I found something online so that I can learn REAL mrdanga beats. My daughter, Sri Priya, keeps telling me my mrdanga is "boring" - but I know that in fact it is offensive to play in unauthorized fashion with literally Balaji. I just didn't know better, and never seem to get any devotee to sit down with me and show me anything. But now I am learning basic mantras - and this makes me very happy. My question is - will you post some more lessons, after lesson 5? I am eager to learn more and more.
Haribol and all glories to the Sankirtana of Sri Krsna Caitanya Mahaprabhu
Janardan dasa
user [255]
next question Mishra,
Are the mantras that are used on "Bhakta Oswald's" lessons, the same as the mantras you use? Are they the "correct" mantras (according to traditional bhajan teaching)? Thanks again.
Janardan
user [2]
I can say for myself as expressed in the site that it is not traditional or professional teaching, but very effective in the practical world of temple bhajan/kirtan indeed.
if you truly learn the 6 basic mantras in the website and chant with them, then I will manage to direct you further.
I will need proof of that, however, a video or something :)
user [247]
is sonding good sounding great. yes, i learned with by te basics, like the word.press linky there, five six basic mantras, but to chant and do beatings iss trickinlgy difficult, so dont mind just practice and practice over musi is like explodes from the practicing out some.
have Fun all, Hare Krsna !!
user [2]
just let me know when you master the beats on the site! Haribol!
user [2]
for our chinese readers that would like to learn mrdanga and cannot reach wordpress blogs:
http://bhaktibox.com/mrdanga.asp
translation courtesy of Vishrambhara Dasa
user [2]
now in spanish:

user [33]
Does anyone know where to get a smaller size mrdanga? My daughter has expressed the desire to learn to play and the full size is too much for her to handle right now.
